channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: White House requests $1.5 trillion for defense spending in 2027 budget
item.description: President Trump released his budget request for 2027 on Friday, asking for $1.5 trillion in defense spending.
item.content:encoded: President Trump released his budget request for 2027 on Friday, asking for $1.5 trillion in defense spending.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with The Atlantic's Nancy Youssef.
item.content:encoded: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with The Atlantic's Nancy Youssef.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Trump removes Pam Bondi as attorney general
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that Pam Bondi will no longer serve as attorney general. Her departure comes amid frustration over her leadership and handling of the Epstein files.
item.content:encoded: President Trump announced Thursday that Pam Bondi will no longer serve as attorney general. Her departure comes amid frustration over her leadership and handling of the Epstein files.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., on the future of the Epstein inquiry with Bondi's ousting
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna of California about the future of the House Oversight Committee's Epstein inquiry, now that Attorney General Pam Bondi is out.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna of California about the future of the House Oversight Committee's Epstein inquiry, now that Attorney General Pam Bondi is out.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Verdicts against Meta and Google may bring a new era of big tech accountability
item.description: Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.
item.content:encoded:
Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit, and 4 astronauts now head to the moon
item.description: With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.
item.content:encoded:
With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi out at the Department of Justice, Iran introduces new toll system for passage through Strait of Hormuz, over 40 countries meet to discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: Attorney General Pam Bondi out at the Department of Justice, Iran introduces new toll system for passage through Strait of Hormuz, over 40 countries meet to discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 'I dreamed it...I expected it': Arsenio Hall talks about his career and new memoir
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with America's beloved late-night TV host Arsenio Hall who writes of the ups and downs of his remarkable career in his new memoir, "Arsenio."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with America's beloved late-night TV host Arsenio Hall who writes of the ups and downs of his remarkable career in his new memoir, "Arsenio."
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Iran wants some ships to pay to use the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has introduced a new toll system for ships it allows to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, asserting its control over the key waterway.
item.content:encoded: Iran has introduced a new toll system for ships it allows to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, asserting its control over the key waterway.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: While others feel the pinch, two countries could benefit from the war in Iran
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Michael McFaul, who served as U.S. ambassador to Russia in the Obama administration, how China and Russia could potentially benefit from the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks Michael McFaul, who served as U.S. ambassador to Russia in the Obama administration, how China and Russia could potentially benefit from the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
